title: Day 12 Interview Prep
description: Practice Context API, immutability, reducer pattern, and state sharing.

questions.question: What is React Context and when do you use it?
keyPoints: Good for theme, auth, cart, locale
questions.sampleAnswer: Context lets me pass data through the component tree without prop drilling. I create a context, wrap subtrees with Provider, and read with useContext. I use it for cart, theme, or auth — data many distant components need. I avoid it for every local UI state.

questions.question: Why must React state updates be immutable?
questions.sampleAnswer: React compares state by reference. Mutating an array in place keeps the same reference — React may not re-render. Creating new objects/arrays signals change. Immutability also makes updates predictable and enables Redux DevTools time travel.

questions.question: What is the reducer pattern in React?
questions.sampleAnswer: useReducer takes a reducer function and initial state. Reducer is pure: (state, action) => newState. Components call dispatch({ type: 'ADD_ITEM', payload }) instead of many setState calls. Cart logic with add/remove/quantity fits reducers well.

questions.question: How do you implement ADD_ITEM when the product may already be in the cart?
keyPoints: Append new item if not found
questions.sampleAnswer: In ADD_ITEM case, check state.items.find(i => i.id === payload.id). If exists, return items mapped — matching id gets { ...item, quantity: item.quantity + 1 }. If not, append { ...payload, quantity: 1 } with spread. Never push to existing array.

questions.question: How do you type cart actions with TypeScript?
questions.sampleAnswer: CartAction is a union: { type: 'ADD_ITEM'; payload: Product } | { type: 'REMOVE_ITEM'; payload: { id: number } } | etc. Reducer switch on action.type — TypeScript narrows payload. CartItem has id, title, price, quantity. CartState holds items array.

questions.question: Cart works on cart page but navbar badge shows 0. Debug steps?
keyPoints: Check both components inside same Provider
Badge computes from state.items not local state
questions.sampleAnswer: Likely navbar is outside CartProvider or reads local state instead of context. I verify CartProvider wraps both routes in main.tsx. Navbar uses useCart().state.items.reduce for count. React DevTools shows context value updating on dispatch.

questions.question: Context causes re-renders in unrelated components. How do you optimize?
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Context performance awareness before Week 3 Redux.
questions.sampleAnswer: All useContext consumers re-render when provider value changes. I split CartStateContext and CartDispatchContext so components needing only dispatch do not re-render on item changes. useMemo stabilizes value object. For large apps, Week 3 Redux or selector libraries help further.

questions.question: Reducer mutates state.items.push but UI sometimes updates. Is this OK?
keyPoints: Mutation is never acceptable in reducer
questions.sampleAnswer: Never OK in production reducer. Mutation may appear to work when React batches or same reference accidentally triggers other updates — fragile and breaks Strict Mode double-invoke. I refactor to return new state with spread/map. Reducers must be pure and immutable.
redFlags: Mutation works so it is fine
